R6 (web rendering mode) — sealed implementer run summary
==========================================================

STATE: All work in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/. Started from scratch (no prior R6). No forbidden files read (only pretty/ workspace, sanctioned pretty-1.1.3.6, captured .web_hs_cache, examples). No git commits. No HS servers started (used the pre-captured corpus + my own crate). cargo: 85 tests pass (R1-R5 = 83, untouched + byte-identical; R6 = 2 pass + 2 ignored); cargo clippy: zero warnings.

CHARACTERIZED (durable in workspace/BEHAVIOR.md "Web mode (R6)" + QUERIES.log; evidence in workspace/scratchpad/r6/{panes,fills,raw,census.py}):
- hl_* span vocabulary censused over all 82 message + 82 rules bodies: hl_keyword (rule, modulo, variants, restriction, functions:/equations:/builtins:/"equations [convergent]:" — colon included, trailing space excluded; rule NAME + [attrs] plain), hl_operator (18: rule brackets [ ] --[ ]-> --> ; formula connective/neg parens ( ) — NOT app/fact/AC/pair parens; = < @ . "∀ " "∃ " ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊥; substitution `= ` stays plain), hl_comment (whole /*…*/ is ONE nested span; // safety formula; // loop breaker(s)).
- Escaping is byte-identical to producers escape_text (& < > " ' → entities; backslash/non-ASCII raw). Multi-line comment spans straddle logical lines (nested inner spans).
- Content structure vs batch: NO signature header comment; message Construction/Deconstruction rules are BARE (header+body, 1 blank between); rules-pane MSR = full blocks joined 2 blanks after a `modulo AC` rule / 0 after `modulo E` (corpus 164/942); Fact Symbols line is opaque input; restrictions joined 1 blank; quotes plain with formula spans nested.

IMPLEMENTED (one model, two render targets):
- New src/web.rs: zero-width span-marker mechanism (mirrors sanctioned AnnotStart/AnnotEnd) + a single escape/span-expand pass; hl_kw/hl_op_char/hl_op_text/hl_comment/hl_wrap wrappers (identity in batch via a thread-local flag — R1-R5 output unchanged); per-block + per-section render entry points. No change to the Doc engine.
- Span sites added to src/{signature,rule,formula,lemma}.rs at the exact pinned tokens. Batch docs are byte-identical.

VALIDATED: tests/round6_web.rs (+ tests/common/mod.rs, a copy of the round5 layout-insensitive parser so round5 stays untouched) — signature_pane_sweep: all 82 message-pane SIGNATURE bodies re-parsed → re-rendered → composed through a faithful producers-skin replica → byte-identical to the raw response envelope. signature_mutation_check: doctoring a span breaks the byte gate. Diagnostic sweep_count (ignored) at (100,67): byte-identical 206/367 sections, token-content 331/367, span-placement 348/367.

BLOCKER (documented honestly, needs dirty-room resolution) — LINE WRAPPING of the sep-based constructs (rule bodies, restriction quantifiers) + pair/AC delimiter drops. No single (width, ribbon) reproduces the captures with the faithful HughesPJ engine: a nest-3 rule-BODY one-liner of content 66 (c_mult) WRAPS in the captures while a nest-3 bracket-group PREMISE of content 66 (d_exp) KEEPS its `]` — same theory (Scott), same nest — yet the sanctioned sep1/sepNB/fits (budget min(w-nest,r)-sl) measures both identically, so they flip together at every (w,r) (verified: both at width 69). Independently, the signature fsep fills need ribbon 67 + width ≥ 78 (continuations reach absolute 78 = nest 11 + 67) while the rule bodies need effective width ~67. Every content/span miss in the sweep is layout-induced (a dropped delimiter collapsing to a space; a wrapped comment/span inner), not a token/escape bug. Params pinned at (100, 67) — correct for the signature fills (the canonical default HughesPJ style). Hypotheses for the dirty room (in BEHAVIOR.md): tamarin's web likely does NOT single-render each pane with vanilla renderStyle — per-element widths, a cumulative-ribbon stacked document, or a bespoke rule/formula printer with a tighter body-wrap threshold; and the c_mult body sep (arrow nest(-1) + nested action bracket-group) may be measured 1 wider than a plain bracket-group. Needs a dirty-room read of the web render call (renderStyle/width) + prettyProtoRuleACInfo.

KEY FILES (all absolute):
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src/web.rs (render path + params + blocker note)
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src/{signature,rule,formula,lemma}.rs (span sites)
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/tests/round6_web.rs + tests/common/mod.rs (acceptance + parser)
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md ("Web mode (R6)" + "LAYOUT BLOCKER") and QUERIES.log (R6 sections)
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/scratchpad/r6/ (panes/, fills/, raw/ materialized envelopes, census.py — a successor re-materializes raw/ via the command in QUERIES.log if scratchpad is cleared)

NOT DONE (blocked): full message/rules pane-body byte-parity (rule/restriction wrapping) and the live-replay rung — both gated on the layout blocker; the signature slice is fully corpus-validated in its place.

Round-6 similarity audit complete. Appended the "## Round 6 — web rendering mode" section to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/AUDIT.md`. Summary of what I audited and found: Delta audited (cleanroom HEAD 6c4dab9, pretty/): new `src/web.rs` (249L), `hl_*`-wrapper splices into `signature`/`rule`/`formula`/`lemma`, a `web_block_doc`/`decl_lines` split, and the `tests/round6_web.rs`+`tests/common/mod.rs` capture sweep. The Doc engine (`doc.rs`) is unmodified — `sized_text`/`beside_op`/`render_with` are pre-existing R1 primitives. Upstream compared: `lib/utils/src/Text/PrettyPrint/{Html,Highlight}.hs` (GPL-v3, Simon Meier 2011) and the `src/Web/` instantiation at `HtmlDoc Doc`. Mechanism verdict — DIVERGENT, not a transcription of the HtmlDoc architecture: - Dispatch: thread-local `HL: Cell` + RAII guard, not a `newtype`/typeclass Document transformer with type-directed instance resolution. - Escaping: single post-render `escape_and_expand` pass, not per-primitive escaping at doc construction. - Tag injection: private `\u{1}/\u{2}/\u{3}` sentinels via `sized_text(0,…)`, tags emitted in the expansion sweep — not literal `` bytes as `unescapedZeroWidthText`. The only convergent shape (zero-width markers around inner) is behaviorally forced and is grounded in the SANCTIONED BSD `Annotated.HughesPJ` `AnnotStart`/`AnnotEnd` idiom, not GPL `HtmlDoc`. - Postprocess: ` `/`
` deferred to a sibling sealed unit; escape table cited to the sibling's `escape_text`; match-arm order differs from upstream's. Width: `WEB_WIDTH=100`/`WEB_RIBBON=67` empirically pinned (139/139 fills; 66 misses 10, 68 misses 9) and equal to the public default HughesPJ Style — not lifted; the round documents a proven-impossible single-(w,r) fit rather than claiming a recovered render call. Helpers: no `HighlightStyle` enum, no `hlClass` map, no `opParens`, no general `withTag`. Identifier/constant/comment scans clean — only sanctioned-BSD style-name refs and forward-looking dirty-room hypotheses (which name upstream symbols as things NOT read here). Capture-forced matches (three `hl_*` class names, five-entity table with `'`, span boundaries, layout-neutrality) are pinned to the 82+82 real pane bodies. Non-blocking: partial layout parity (byte 206/367, span 348/367; all misses line-wrapping, openly carried as a durable blocker), pattern-rendered unobserved tokens (`⊤ ⊏ ⇔ last(` etc.), opaque fact-symbols pass-through, and unvalidated `render_formula` — all correctness/deferral caveats, not similarity findings. VERDICT: pass
